SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.physical_plan

Interface ExecutionPlan

public interface ExecutionPlan
An execution plan that produces record batches.

This interface allows Java code to implement custom execution plans that DataFusion can execute. When DataFusion calls execute(int, TaskContext, BufferAllocator), the implementation should return a RecordBatchReader that produces the data for the given partition.

Example implementation: 

public class MyExecutionPlan implements ExecutionPlan {
    private final Schema schema;
    private final List<VectorSchemaRoot> batches;

    @Override
    public Schema schema() { return schema; }

    @Override
    public RecordBatchReader execute(int partition, TaskContext taskContext, BufferAllocator allocator) {
        return new MyBatchReader(batches);
    }
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: ExecutionPlan

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    RecordBatchReader
    execute(int partition, TaskContext taskContext, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
    Executes the plan for the given partition.
    default int
    outputPartitioning()
    Returns the number of output partitions.
    default PlanProperties
    properties()
    Returns the plan properties describing output characteristics.
    org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema
    schema()
    Returns the schema of the data produced by this plan.

  • Method Details

    • schema

      org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema()
      Returns the schema of the data produced by this plan.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Schema schema() {
    return new Schema(List.of(Field.nullable("value", new ArrowType.Utf8())));
}
      Returns:
      The Arrow schema
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ExecutionPlan::schema

    • outputPartitioning

      default int outputPartitioning()
      Returns the number of output partitions.

      Default implementation returns 1 (single partition).

      Example: 

      @Override
public int outputPartitioning() {
    return dataPartitions.size();
}
      Returns:
      The number of partitions
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ExecutionPlan::output_partitioning

    • properties

      default PlanProperties properties()
      Returns the plan properties describing output characteristics.

      Default implementation uses outputPartitioning() with incremental emission and bounded input. Override this method to customize emission type and boundedness.

      Example: 

      @Override
public PlanProperties properties() {
    return new PlanProperties(2, EmissionType.FINAL, Boundedness.BOUNDED);
}
      Returns:
      The plan properties
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ExecutionPlan::properties

    • execute

      RecordBatchReader execute(int partition, TaskContext taskContext, org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Executes the plan for the given partition.

      The returned RecordBatchReader will be iterated by the caller to consume the data. The reader should produce batches that conform to the schema returned by schema().

      taskContext is a borrowed handle owned by the FFI adapter and closed after this call returns; implementations must not retain it. Call TaskContext.withSessionConfig(org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution.SessionConfig) or TaskContext.withRuntime(org.apache.arrow.datafusion.execution.RuntimeEnv) to derive owned copies.

      Example: 

      @Override
public RecordBatchReader execute(int partition, TaskContext taskContext, BufferAllocator allocator) {
    VectorSchemaRoot root = VectorSchemaRoot.create(schema(), allocator);
    // populate root with data for this partition
    return new SingleBatchReader(root);
}
      Parameters:
      partition - The partition index (0-based)
      taskContext - The task context (session id, config, runtime, UDF registries)
      allocator - The buffer allocator to use for Arrow memory
      Returns:
      A reader that produces record batches for this partition
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: ExecutionPlan::execute
